title: Java Data-Structures Course Project
summary: A data-structures course repository with two parts: six lab experiments (Experiments/) and fifteen homework assignments (Homework/). Every module is a standalone Java compilation unit. Edges reflect curriculum prerequisites: conceptual dependencies where one module's algorithm directly builds on structures or traversal patterns introduced in another.
instructionPreview: This is a Java data-structures course project with two parts: six lab experiments (`Experiments/`) and fifteen homework assignments (`Homework/`). Most core algorithm functions have been stubbed out with `throw new UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")` and you must restore their implementations so that the project compiles and all tests pass.
instruction:
This is a Java data-structures course project with two parts: six lab
experiments (`Experiments/`) and fifteen homework assignments (`Homework/`).
Most core algorithm functions have been stubbed out with
`throw new UnsupportedOperationException("TODO: implement")` and you must
restore their implementations so that the project compiles and all tests pass.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The full source tree is already present
under `/workspace/Experiments/` and `/workspace/Homework/`.

## Modules to Implement

### Experiments

- **DSExp01_Stack** (`Experiments/DSExp01_Stack/`): Stack-based infix/postfix
  expression processing. Converts infix expressions (with +, -, *, /, ^,
  parentheses, and variable symbols) to postfix notation, tokenises expression
  strings, inserts implicit multiplication for variable terms, and evaluates
  postfix expressions numerically. Reads from `samples.txt`. (~2 files,
  ~4 functions to implement)

- **DSExp02_Tree** (`Experiments/DSExp02_Tree/`): Binary-tree construction and
  traversal. Builds a binary tree from user input, supports recursive and
  iterative pre-order/in-order/post-order traversals, level-order traversal,
  tree-property classification (full/complete/balanced), and lowest-common-ancestor
  queries. (~1 file, ~13 functions to implement)

- **DSExp03_Graph** (`Experiments/DSExp03_Graph/`): Graph construction and
  traversal on both adjacency-matrix and adjacency-list representations. Builds
  structures from an edge-list data file, converts between representations,
  and performs recursive/iterative DFS and BFS from every vertex. Reads from
  `data.txt`. (~1 file, ~15 functions to implement)

- **DSExp04_Search** (`Experiments/DSExp04_Search/`): Binary-search-tree
  operations and binary-search analysis. Builds BSTs from ordered and random
  data files, supports insertion, deletion (with delete-min), search, and
  in-order sort. Also builds a half-search (decision-tree) structure for sorted
  arrays, and computes average successful/unsuccessful search lengths for both
  BST and half-search. Reads from `order.txt` and `random.txt`. (~1 file,
  ~21 functions to implement)

- **DSExp05_Hash** (`Experiments/DSExp05_Hash/`): Consistent hashing with
  virtual nodes. Generates server IP addresses, maps them onto a hash ring using
  a hash function (FNV-like), supports adding/removing servers and looking up
  which server handles a given key. (~1 file, ~7 functions to implement)

- **DSExp05_RedBlackTree** (`Experiments/DSExp05_RedBlackTree/`): Red-black tree
  with full insert/delete/rebalance. Builds a red-black tree from `order.txt`,
  supports left/right rotations, post-insert and post-delete rebalancing,
  search, in-order traversal, and average search-length analysis.
  (~1 file, ~13 functions to implement)

### Homework

- **DS01** (`Homework/DS01/`): Four standalone programs — custom-comparator
  quicksort for string arrays, two-pointer maximum-finding in arrays, DFS-based
  Hamiltonian-path search on a graph, and a sports-competition scoring/query
  system with file serialisation. (~4 files, ~14 functions to implement)

- **DS02_LinkedList** (`Homework/DS02_LinkedList/`): Product inventory management
  via a sorted linked list. Supports adding, deleting, searching (by name/brand),
  updating, and file-based persistence. (~1 file, ~11 functions to implement)

- **DS02_Matrix** (`Homework/DS02_Matrix/`): Sparse-matrix transposition using
  triple (row, col, value) representation. Implements both a naive O(cols*n)
  transposition and a fast O(n) transposition with pre-computed column counts
  and start positions. (~2 files, ~5 functions to implement)

- **DS02_String** (`Homework/DS02_String/`): Pattern-matching on a linked-list
  representation of strings. Constructs a character-level linked list from input
  and performs substring matching against it. (~1 file, ~2 functions to implement)

- **DS03_Huffman** (`Homework/DS03_Huffman/`): Huffman-coding file compression
  and decompression. Reads a text file, computes character frequencies, builds
  a Huffman tree, encodes content to a binary file with a key file, and decodes
  back. (~1 file, ~11 functions to implement)

- **DS03_LoserTree** (`Homework/DS03_LoserTree/`): K-way merge sort using a
  loser tree. Initialises K sorted runs, builds the loser tree, and repeatedly
  extracts the overall minimum to produce a fully sorted output.
  (~1 file, ~4 functions to implement)

- **DS03_PriorityQueue** (`Homework/DS03_PriorityQueue/`): Max-heap priority
  queue. Reads tasks from a data file, supports heap insertion with upward
  percolation and extracting the maximum with downward percolation.
  (~1 file, ~3 functions to implement)

- **DS04_Farmer** (`Homework/DS04_Farmer/`): Farmer-wolf-goat-cabbage river-
  crossing puzzle solver. Builds a state-space graph where each node is a
  4-tuple of positions, validates states, connects valid transitions, and uses
  DFS to find all solutions. (~1 file, ~6 functions to implement)

- **DS04_IslandLake** (`Homework/DS04_IslandLake/`): Minimum-spanning-tree
  bridge construction using Kruskal's algorithm with union-find on a set of
  islands with given inter-island distances. (~1 file, ~2 functions to implement)

- **DS04_Maze** (`Homework/DS04_Maze/`): Maze generation and solving with
  graphical rendering. Generates random mazes via DFS-based wall carving and
  Prim's algorithm, then solves them with DFS pathfinding. Uses Swing for
  visualisation. (~1 file, ~9 functions to implement)

- **DS04_Project** (`Homework/DS04_Project/`): Activity-on-edge (AOE) network
  for project scheduling. Builds an AOE graph from a data file, displays the
  adjacency list, computes earliest/latest event times, and finds the critical
  path via DFS enumeration. (~1 file, ~5 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called by the test suite (indirectly via compiling
and running the Java programs). You must implement them with these exact
signatures and behaviors:

- `DSExperiment.convertToPostfix(String infixExpression) -> String` — converts infix to space-separated postfix notation
- `DSExperiment.calculatePostfixExpression(String postfixExpression) -> double` — evaluates a postfix expression numerically
- `DSExperiment.addMultiply(String infixExpression) -> String` — inserts implicit '*' between coefficients and variables
- `DSExperiment.splitIntoPieces(String infixExpression) -> ArrayList<String>` — tokenises an infix expression string into operands and operators
- `DSExp03 Main.DFS2(int i) -> void` — recursive DFS step on adjacency matrix, prints vertex, marks visited, records DFN
- `DSExp04 Main.insertBST(NodeType node, NodeType parent, int value) -> void` — recursive BST insertion
- `DSExp04 Main.searchBST(NodeType node, int value) -> int` — recursive BST search, returns value or -1
- `DSExp04 Main.sortBST(NodeType node) -> void` — in-order traversal appending to allElements list
- `DSExp05_RedBlackTree Main.insertBST(TreeNode node, int record) -> TreeNode` — BST placement for red-black tree
- `DSExp05_RedBlackTree Main.balanceTree(TreeNode targetNode) -> void` — post-insertion red-black rebalancing

Each experiment reads from data files already present in its directory
(e.g., `samples.txt`, `data.txt`, `order.txt`, `random.txt`). Compile each
experiment's Java source files individually from their respective directories
(use `LANG=C.UTF-8 javac` to avoid filename encoding errors from Chinese-named
non-source files).

moduleDag.description: A data-structures course repository with two parts: six lab experiments (Experiments/) and fifteen homework assignments (Homework/). Every module is a standalone Java compilation unit. Edges reflect curriculum prerequisites: conceptual dependencies where one module's algorithm directly builds on structures or traversal patterns introduced in another.

nodes.label: DSExp01 — Stack & Expression Processing
nodes.description: Self-contained stack used to convert infix expressions to postfix notation, tokenise expression strings, insert implicit multiplication for variable terms, and evaluate postfix expressions numerically. Reads from samples.txt.

nodes.label: DS01 — Basic Algorithm Programs
nodes.description: Four standalone programs: custom-comparator quicksort for string arrays, two-pointer maximum-finding in arrays, DFS-based Hamiltonian-path search, and a sports-competition scoring/query system with file serialisation.

nodes.label: DS02 LinkedList — Product Inventory
nodes.description: Sorted linked list for product inventory management: add, delete, search (by name/brand), update, and file-based persistence.

nodes.label: DS02 Matrix — Sparse Matrix Transposition
nodes.description: Sparse-matrix transposition using triple (row, col, value) representation. Implements a naive O(cols·n) transposition and a fast O(n) version with pre-computed column counts and start positions.

nodes.label: DS03 PriorityQueue — Max-Heap
nodes.description: Max-heap priority queue reading tasks from a data file; supports heap insertion with upward percolation and maximum extraction with downward percolation.

nodes.label: DSExp05 Hash — Consistent Hashing
nodes.description: Consistent hashing with virtual nodes: generates server IPs, maps them onto a hash ring using an FNV-like function, and supports adding/removing servers and key-to-server lookup.

nodes.label: DSExp02 Tree — Binary Tree Traversal
nodes.description: Binary tree built from pre-order user input. Supports recursive and iterative pre/in/post-order and level-order traversals, tree-property classification (full/complete/balanced), and lowest-common-ancestor queries.

nodes.label: DS02 String — Linked-List Pattern Matching
nodes.description: Constructs a character-level linked list from input and performs substring pattern matching against it. Requires the linked-list mechanics established in DS02_LinkedList.

nodes.label: DSExp03 Graph — Adjacency-Matrix & Adjacency-List Traversal
nodes.description: Builds directed/undirected graphs from an edge-list data file in both adjacency-matrix and adjacency-list representations, converts between them, and runs recursive/iterative DFS and BFS from every vertex.

nodes.label: DSExp04 Search — BST & Half-Search
nodes.description: Builds BSTs from ordered and random data files; supports insertion, deletion (with delete-min), search, and in-order sort. Also builds a half-search (decision-tree) structure and computes average successful/unsuccessful search lengths for both approaches.

nodes.label: DS03 Huffman — File Compression
nodes.description: Huffman-coding file compressor/decompressor: computes character frequencies, builds a Huffman tree via greedy priority-queue selection, encodes content to a binary file with a key file, and decodes back.

nodes.label: DS03 LoserTree — K-way Merge Sort
nodes.description: K-way merge sort using a loser-tree tournament structure: initialises K sorted runs, builds the loser tree, and repeatedly extracts the overall minimum to produce fully sorted output.

nodes.label: DSExp05 Red-Black Tree
nodes.description: Red-black tree with full insert/delete/rebalance. Supports left/right rotations, post-insert and post-delete colour rebalancing, in-order traversal, BST search, and average search-length analysis. Requires a correct BST implementation from DSExp04.

nodes.label: DS04 Farmer — River-Crossing Puzzle
nodes.description: Models the farmer-wolf-goat-cabbage puzzle as a state-space graph (nodes are 4-tuples of positions), validates transitions, and uses DFS to enumerate all solutions.

nodes.label: DS04 IslandLake — Kruskal MST
nodes.description: Minimum-spanning-tree bridge construction using Kruskal's algorithm with union-find on a set of islands with given inter-island distances.

nodes.label: DS04 Maze — Generation & Solving
nodes.description: Random maze generator and solver: DFS-based wall carving and Prim's algorithm for generation, DFS pathfinding for solving. Uses Swing for graphical visualisation.

nodes.label: DS04 Project — AOE Network & Critical Path
nodes.description: Activity-on-Edge network for project scheduling: builds an AOE graph, computes earliest/latest event times, and finds critical paths via DFS enumeration of zero-slack activities.

edges.label: DS02_String represents strings as character-level linked lists — it requires the linked-list traversal mechanics implemented in DS02_LinkedList
Graph DFS/BFS is a direct generalisation of recursive tree traversal; implementing binary-tree pre/in-order traversal first makes the DFS/BFS visitor pattern straightforward to adapt to graphs
A BST is a binary tree with the search-order property; BST insertion, deletion, and search build directly on the node-pointer tree structure from DSExp02
A red-black tree is a self-balancing BST; the insertBST placement step and post-insert colour rebalancing both require a solid, correct BST from DSExp04
Huffman tree construction greedily selects the two lowest-frequency nodes, which requires a working priority-queue (min-heap) whose insert/extract semantics are established in DS03_PriorityQueue

The loser tree is an alternative priority-queue structure for K-way merging; understanding upward/downward percolation from DS03_PriorityQueue makes the loser-tree winner/loser update semantics concrete
The river-crossing puzzle is solved with DFS on a state-space graph — the same DFS pattern used on both adjacency-matrix and adjacency-list graphs in DSExp03
Kruskal's MST algorithm operates on an edge-weighted graph; the graph construction and edge-iteration patterns from DSExp03 are direct prerequisites
Maze generation (DFS wall-carving, Prim's) and solving (DFS pathfinding) are graph algorithms on a grid graph, building on the DFS/BFS implementation from DSExp03
An AOE network is a DAG; computing earliest/latest times and enumerating critical paths requires the graph construction and DFS traversal established in DSExp03
